titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/236

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=200 status=live modified=2017-03-02 18:17:04.298971+00 | translation moa=201 status=live modified=2017-03-02 18:17:06.917881+00

div.p:
.W. dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue Salutem. Sciatis me concessisse
abbati ⁊ Conuentui de Sancta cruce ut nullus super eos aut super homines suos namum capiat
nisi pro dominicis debitis suis aut pro debitis hominum suorum . Quare prohibeo firmiter ne quis
uel in Scotia . uel apud Insulam de May . cum homines predicti Abbatis illuc ad piscandum uenerint  super
canonicos de sancta cruce aut super eorum homines namum capiat nisi sit pro dominicis debi
tis suis . aut pro debitis hominum suorum . Super meam forisfacturam . Testibus . Hug’ . Cancellario
meo . Ric’ . de Prebenda clerico meo Apud Hadint’.

William by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of the whole of his land, Greeting: Know that I have granted to the abbot and convent of Holyrood that no-one may take poinds from them or from their men unless for their own debts or for the debts of their men. Wherefore I firmly forbid that anyone either in Scotia, or on the Isle of May when the men of the aforesaid abbot come there to fish, may take poinds from the canons of Holyrood or from their men unless it be for their demesne debts, or for the debts of their men, on pain of our forfeiture. As witnesses, Hugh my chancellor, Richard de Prebenda my clerk. At Haddington.
